title: Ingress, Gateway API 1.5.1, Routing, TLS, Delegation, and Status · Mastery Check
summary: A release review compares fresh ingress, gateway api 1.5.1, routing, tls, delegation, and status choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Gateway API routing and delegation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.
Select GatewayClass and implementation from controller identity, parameters, supported features, conformance reports, release channel, and operational ownership.
Design Gateway addresses and listeners with protocol, port, hostname, namespace attachment, certificate reference, TLS mode, and conflict behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary orchestration evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, keep K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Gateway API 1.5.1 route is not Accepted, a cross-namespace backend lacks permission, two rules conflict, and TLS terminates with the wrong certificate. Establish controller support, attachment, ReferenceGrant, matching, status, and conformance evidence. Competency probe: Compare Service, Ingress, implementation-specific annotations, Gateway API, controller installation, data plane, and conformance boundaries.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Creating an Ingress or Gateway automatically installs a conformant traffic controller.

options.text: Capture client and server versions, context, namespace, API discovery, current object state, ownership, and immutable release identity for the quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary case
Predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, security, disruption, and cleanup effects for the quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary case
Review the smallest manifest, Kustomize, Helm, policy, or runbook increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary case
Reject one failure, record repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable-cluster transfer evidence for the quiz K8s Ingress Gateway Boundary case
